ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class (FOODON_03315873). No exact class for pork-and-cabbage filled boiled dumplings with spicy sesame sauce exists; closest_parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers filled pasta/dumplings broadly. No specific code for Chinese shuijiao with pork-cabbage filling and sesame sauce exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024; closest_parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for Chinese pork-and-cabbage dumplings with spicy sesame sauce.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of handmade pork and cabbage dumplings is approximately 8–10 pieces (about 200 g), served with spicy sesame sauce on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Pork and Cabbage Dumplings (Shui Jiao) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for handmade pork and cabbage dumplings with spicy sesame sauce (cr_id G00010052). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for pork and cabbage dumplings (FDC ID 2103705) as these fields are not reported in the HPB record; adaptation is noted.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for handmade pork-and-cabbage dumplings with spicy sesame sauce. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on the closest analogous food: boiled wheat-flour dumplings (jiaozi/wonton-type), which have a reported GI of approximately 46–55 in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The moderate protein (8.7 g/100g) and fat (3.78 g/100g) content from the pork filling further attenuates glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI (48) × carbohydrate per serving (16.06 g from 200 g serving) / 100 = 7.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content elevated when spicy sesame sauce (containing soy sauce and chilli oil) is included; base dumpling sodium is low at 46 mg/100g but sauce substantially increases total sodium load.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request the spicy sesame sauce on the side and use no more than one teaspoon, or substitute with a low-sodium dipping sauce, to keep total sodium within safe limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate content (8 g/100g) with negligible sugar (0.21 g/100g); protein (8.71 g/100g) and fat (3.78 g/100g) from pork filling slow gastric emptying and attenuate postprandial glucose rise; estimated GI of 48 (low).
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes may consume a moderate portion of 6–8 dumplings (approximately 150 g) with sauce used sparingly, monitoring for any added sugar in the sesame sauce preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Pork filling contributes saturated fat (~1.2 g/100g) and cholesterol (28 mg/100g); sesame paste provides beneficial MUFA and vitamin E, but chilli oil and soy sauce in the sauce increase sodium and potentially saturated fat intake.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 6–8 dumplings, request sauce on the side, and choose preparations using lean pork mince to reduce saturated fat and cholesterol intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork filling contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) which can amplify pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways; chilli is a nightshade ingredient that may trigger sensitivity in some individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting pork filling with chicken or tofu, and exercise caution with chilli-containing sauce if nightshade sensitivity is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Handmade Pork and Cabbage Dumplings with Spicy Sesame Sauce (鲜肉白菜手工水饺配芝麻辣酱) are traditional Chinese boiled dumplings (shuijiao) prepared by hand-wrapping a seasoned filling of minced premium pork and crisp Tianjin cabbage inside thin wheat-flour wrappers, then boiling until tender. The dumplings are served with a spicy sesame sauce made from sesame paste (zhima jiang), chilli oil, soy sauce, garlic, and vinegar, which adds a rich, nutty, and pungent heat to each bite. The texture is soft and yielding with a juicy, savoury filling. This dish is a staple of Northern Chinese cuisine and is widely available in Singapore's Chinese food establishments. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate with negligible sugar (0.21 g). The protein content from pork and the moderate fat from sesame paste contribute to satiety and a low estimated glycaemic index of approximately 48. Sodium in the dumpling base is low (46 mg/100g) but increases substantially when the spicy sesame sauce is consumed; patients with hypertension should use the sauce sparingly. The dish is suitable in moderate portions for individuals managing type 2 diabetes due to its low GI and high protein content. Cholesterol is present at 28 mg/100g from the pork filling, which is relevant for cardiovascular disease management. Iron content is notable at 1.79 mg/100g, contributing to daily iron requirements.
common_user_queries: How many calories in handmade dumplings?
What is the GI of Chinese dumplings?
Are pork and cabbage dumplings suitable for hypertension?
How much protein in pork dumplings?
What allergens are in dumplings with sesame sauce?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010052) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue (pork and cabbage dumplings) and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (boiled wheat-flour dumplings) with clear documentation. All mandatory multilingual names present for a Singaporean Chinese dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin profile, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific handmade pork-and-cabbage dumpling preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010052) or the USDA FoodData Central closest analogue entry as of 2026. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100g) and dietary fibre (0.8 g/100g) are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for pork and cabbage dumplings (FDC ID 2103705) as these fields were absent in the HPB source record. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on boiled wheat-flour dumplings from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008), Diabetes Care; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists in approved sources. The sodium value of 46 mg/100g reflects the dumpling base only as recorded in HPB; the full dish with spicy sesame sauce will have substantially higher sodium. Halal status is not confirmed as pork is a primary ingredient.
